To try this season
Offline
Works when Wi-Fi drops
At the gate

No line at the sign-up table.

Drivers pull up RaceDay on their own phone, type their name — or tap it if they've raced with you before — and pick their classes. Their heat pill draws automatically.

Heats build themselves the moment sign-up closes: pill order, inside/outside lines, B-mains and features, all laid out and ready to post before the drivers' meeting.

Plug any TV into the wall. RaceDay fills the screen with rotating lineups — no crowding the tower to read a printout.
After the checkers

Enter finishes. Points update themselves.

Pick each driver's finish from a dropdown — or import a CSV straight from your timing system. Standings, the B-main, and the feature lineup all build from those numbers automatically.

Season points update the instant a feature is scored. No Monday-morning spreadsheet, no re-adding a season by hand.

Everything else

Organized, not just fast.

A driver book that remembers every racer year to year. Backups you can export anytime. A full audit trail of every result edit. Race-day history you can pull up months later.

Runs in a browser on whatever laptop or tablet you already own — nothing to install, nothing to configure on a server.

Introducing Driven

One profile.
Every track you race.

Drivers build a free Driven profile once — name, kart number, sponsors, career stats — and it follows them to every track running RaceDay. At sign-up they just hand over a short link code instead of retyping everything.

Back it up anytime, restore it on a new phone in seconds, and share a public card that fans and tracks can pull up. It's the driver's identity, not RaceDay's — they keep it wherever they race.

See Driven →
Why it's free to try

I built RaceDay because I wanted a race night to run off a laptop instead of a clipboard.

It's new for this season, so I'm not going to pretend a dozen tracks are already running it — you'd be one of the first. What that means for you: every class gets set up by hand with you, and I'm reachable on your first race night if something doesn't behave.

Free trial, no card, no pressure. If it earns its keep, we'll talk about a license that costs less than one night's gate.

Already use paper?
Keep it as backup the first night.
Bad Wi-Fi at the track?
Runs fully offline on the sign-up device.
Use a timing system?
RaceDay isn't timing — it's everything around it, and it imports your CSVs.
Fans pay for the app?
Never. Spectators watch live lineups free — a QR code, no app, no subscription.
— Josh
